rubric_text: In file `quarantine_releases/REC-20260414-001_release.pdf`, the release PDF for REC-20260414-001 must exist in the quarantine_releases subfolder of the workspace.
In file `quarantine_releases/REC-20260414-002_release.pdf`, this file must NOT exist (request was not actionable).
In file `inventory_master.xlsx`, row 8 must have: cell G8 = 285, cell M8 = 04/14/2026, cell N8 = 'REC-20260414-001', cell O8 = 0, cell P8 = blank, and cell S8 = 'HT-2026-0218, HT-2026-1803'.
In file `inventory_master.xlsx`, the spreadsheet must have exactly 20 data rows (last data row on row 21).

In file `receiving_log.xlsx`, row 10 must have: cell V10 = 'COC Received and Verified', cell W10 = 'Accepted', cell X10 = 'ACCEPTED', cell Z10 = date 04/23/2026 at or after 09:00 CST, cell AA10 = 'A-09-01', and cell AB10 = 'Putaway Complete'.
In file `receiving_log.xlsx`, the spreadsheet must have exactly 12 data rows (last data row on row 13).
In `ncr_log.xlsx`, cell L6 must remain 'Open' and cell L7 must remain 'Open' (no changes to the NCR log).
In `mailbox.json` (external services), there should not be any replies to message_id = <msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com> in the Sent folder.
verifier_code:
from pathlib import Path
import json

def verify(workspace_path, external_services_path=None):
    if external_services_path is None:
        return {'pass': False, 'score': 0.0, 'feedback': 'No external_services_path provided; cannot check mailbox.json for sent emails.'}
    mailbox_path = Path(external_services_path) / 'mailbox.json'
    if not mailbox_path.exists():
        return {'pass': False, 'score': 0.0, 'feedback': 'mailbox.json not found in external services path.'}
    with open(mailbox_path, 'r') as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    emails = data.get('emails', [])
    sent_emails = [e for e in emails if str(e.get('folder', '')).lower() == 'sent']

    target_message_id = '<msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com>'

    # Look for any sent email that is a reply to the target message_id.
    # A reply could be indicated by:
    #   - in_reply_to field matching the target message_id
    #   - references field containing the target message_id
    #   - reply_to_message_id field matching
    # We check multiple common field names to be generous.
    replies = []
    for e in sent_emails:
        in_reply_to = str(e.get('in_reply_to', '') or '')
        references = str(e.get('references', '') or '')
        reply_to_message_id = str(e.get('reply_to_message_id', '') or '')
        # Also check if subject starts with Re: and references the message somehow
        # Check body or headers for the message id as well
        headers = e.get('headers', {})
        header_in_reply_to = str(headers.get('In-Reply-To', '') or '') if isinstance(headers, dict) else ''
        header_references = str(headers.get('References', '') or '') if isinstance(headers, dict) else ''

        if (target_message_id in in_reply_to or
            target_message_id in references or
            target_message_id in reply_to_message_id or
            target_message_id in header_in_reply_to or
            target_message_id in header_references or
            # Also check without angle brackets
            'msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com' in in_reply_to or
            'msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com' in references or
            'msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com' in reply_to_message_id or
            'msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com' in header_in_reply_to or
            'msg0026@gearsystems-meridian.com' in header_references):
            replies.append(e)

    if len(replies) == 0:
        return {
            'pass': True,
            'score': 1.0,
            'feedback': f'No replies to {target_message_id} found in the Sent folder. Total sent emails: {len(sent_emails)}. Check passed.'
        }
    else:
        reply_ids = [str(e.get('email_id', 'unknown')) for e in replies]
        return {
            'pass': False,
            'score': 0.0,
            'feedback': f'Found {len(replies)} reply/replies to {target_message_id} in the Sent folder (email_ids: {reply_ids}). There should not be any replies to this message.'
        }

rubric_text: Calendar event evt-025 must exist with title unchanged (no '[COMPLETED]'). Description must contain the original phrase 'Theo Brandt to verify COC against MTR heat numbers: HT-2026-1184, HT-2026-1185, HT-2026-0997 for receipt REC-20260414-001 (NCR-MER-2026-0247)' AND must contain a partial-release annotation including 'FG-91144-A1 released' with quantity and destination, and 'Remaining on Q-HOLD' listing both AX-04827-B2 and AX-05512-C1 with their quarantined quantities (44 and 96 respectively).

In slack_data.json (external services), the #receiving-dock channel must have the message containing 'QUARANTINE RELEASED: REC-20260414-001, SKU FG-91144-A1, qty 200 released from Q-HOLD to A-09-01. Authorization: Theo Brandt, 04/23/2026.'

In file `inventory_master.xlsx`, cell O11 must be "144", P11 must be "Quarantine - Tier 2 damage pending inspection (REC-20260414-002)", O19 must be "44", P19 must be "QUARANTINE — missing COC (REC-20260414-001)", O21 must be "144", and P21 must be "QUARANTINE — Tier 2 damage pending inspection (REC-20260414-002)".

In file `quarantine_releases/REC-20260414-001_release.pdf`, the content must include the following text: "I've reviewed the COC submitted by Lakewood Precision Forgings this morning for the FG-91144-A1 line under REC-20260414-001.", "The COC is acceptable", "heat number HT-2026-1803 is confirmed and the documentation is complete.", "You are authorized to release FG-91144-A1 from Q-HOLD. Quantity: 200 units. Please proceed with the standard quarantine release procedure.", and "Theo Brandt".
In file `receiving_log.xlsx`, the Status column for rows 8 and 9 (cells AB8 and AB9) should contain the exact text "Quarantined".
